_comment: Labelled corpus for EnumGrid's LIVE-NVD CPE->CVE pipeline (backend/cve.py),
scored by nvd_precision.py --live against the authoritative NVD API 2.0 feed.
Each case is a version-scoped CPE 2.3 match string plus two LABEL sets:
expect_present - CVE ids that MUST be surfaced for this exact version. This is
a LOWER BOUND (recall): NVD returns more, and the extras are
NOT counted as false positives, because a version's full CVE
set is not knowable a priori. Drawn from well-documented,
textbook applicability (the same bugs the testbed plants).
expect_absent  - CVE ids that MUST NOT appear, because the build is patched or
the CPE names a different product. A returned id here is a
recall         - a vulnerable build; expect_present MUST be recalled.
version-scope  - a PATCHED build one past the fix; the CVE MUST NOT appear.
wrong-product  - a different product's CPE; another product's CVE MUST NOT leak.
IMPORTANT: --live against real NVD is authoritative. These labels are a hypothesis
about NVD's published applicability; if the live feed contradicts a label, that is
a FINDING to investigate (a CPE-formatting or NVD-config nuance), never something
to fabricate away. Extras beyond expect_present are expected and unscored.

cases.note: path-traversal in 2.4.49; the flagship planted testbed bug
patched upstream - neither traversal CVE may appear for 2.4.51
SambaCry fixed at 4.6.4 - must not appear
Ghostcat fixed at 9.0.31 - must not appear
cross-product isolation: an OpenSSH CPE must never leak Apache/vsftpd/Samba CVEs
Openfire admin-console auth bypass path traversal, < 4.7.5
